hero.subtitle: Senior Frontend Developer
hero.tagline:
10+ years building web products meant to last.
Rails, React, Vue. Whatever the job needs.

bullets: Maintained and stabilised the Rails codebase as the team's primary Rails resource
Resolved breaking issues and content-critical fixes across live customer-facing pages
Contributed to the migration toward a Next.js architecture
Led the V2 UI overhaul of a physical router firmware product (Vue.js)
Led multiple Rails-to-React migration cycles on ExpressVPN's customer-facing pages
Contributed to a browser-based password manager extension
Contributed to projects credited with driving or saving hundreds of thousands of dollars in revenue
Migrated translation management system from Phrase to Crowdin across the full product
Built the investor-facing frontend for a private equity crowdfunding platform

about.title: About
about.p1: I've spent the better part of a decade building web products for one of the world's largest VPN providers, starting as a junior and ending as the person the team called when something was on fire. Along the way: framework migrations, firmware UIs, browser extensions, and more conversion funnels than I'd care to count.
about.p2: Outside client work, I build games under tuskat (three shipped titles across Phaser and Unity) and utility apps with OttlerStudio, because apparently I find "good UI for boring tools" genuinely interesting. I also do digital illustration, mostly for fun and occasionally for things people can look at.
about.p3: The best work I've done has been in teams where the why mattered as much as the when, where craft was valued, not just velocity. I'm not looking for the next moonshot. I'm looking for a place where good work is its own reward, and where the people around me feel the same.

expressvpn.title: ExpressVPN Web Estate
expressvpn.desc: 10+ years of continuous work on one of the world's largest VPN providers. Customer landing pages, internal CMS, framework migrations from Rails through multiple generations of React, router firmware UI, and a browser-based password manager extension. Projects credited with driving hundreds of thousands in revenue impact.

brise.title: Brise
brise.desc: A self-hosted platform for proxying and orchestrating multiple LLM backends. Persona-driven interface with hyperparameter tuning, multi-turn chat, a single-shot playground, and a SQLite-backed request log with metrics dashboard.

rodent.title: R0D3nt
rodent.desc: A platformer where the odds are deliberately unfair. Too many enemies to fight head-on. Survive by leading them into traps and off ledges instead.

teddysCrew.title: Teddy's Crew
teddysCrew.desc: Top-down arcade shooter with endless mob waves and deliberately simple controls. Built on Phaser 3 with a Vue.js interface layer.

roadToDakar.title: Road to Dakar
roadToDakar.desc: An endless racer inspired by Outrun, but with proper 3D terrain and real car physics, not a side-scroller sliding on a single axis.

ottlerstudio.title: OttlerStudio
ottlerstudio.desc: A collection of single-purpose utility apps built with a friend: Password Generator, Cooking Timer, Flashlight, and more. The premise: do one thing, do it with better UX than what's already on the market. Currently in development; store release pending.

contact.title: Contact
contact.intro: No forms. Just pick your preferred channel.
contact.meta: This portfolio and CV were built in plain HTML/CSS and Astro, no templates. The CV exports to pixel-perfect PDF via a Puppeteer pipeline. Iterated with Claude Code.
